commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: lexify
repository.description: Headless, plugin-driven Rich Text Editor built on Lexical

commit.message:
feat: nav drawer, new themes, eCourt captcha loop fix, bare act improvements

- Add left navigation drawer on home screen (hamburger button top-left)
  - Today's agenda (hearings + tasks) shown inline in drawer
  - All integrations (eCourt, Judgments, Bare Acts, Cloud Sync, Meetings) moved to drawer
- Remove Pending Fees insight card and Financial Health card from home screen
- Remove Record Payment quick action chip from home screen

- Replace app themes: Charcoal, Dark Graphite, High Contrast, Red Graphite (+ keep Onyx)
  - Themes sourced from LexCodex assets
  - Safe fallback in UserPreferencesRepository for renamed enum values

- Fix eCourt captcha loop: dismissEcourtErrorModal() clears stale Bootstrap
  modal before each search attempt
- Auto-refresh captcha after validation_error/no_result/timeout
- Clear captcha input field in ViewModel on error

- Bare Act library: IndiaCode bridge re-injection after page navigation,
  English + Hindi PDF discovery, download flow fixes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Enhance GitHubCopilotProvider with input_items and command execution (#302)

* fix: add input_items parameter to GitHubCopilotProvider.responses

* fix: handle None function_calls in responses

* fix: use tool_calls instead of undefined function_calls in GitHubCopilot

* refactor: reuse _convert_messages_to_input_items in GitHubCopilotProvider

* fix: use self.include_memory instead of undefined variable

* feat: add paste-to-upload images and inline image display in chat

* feat: generate today's daily memory and always overwrite

* refactor: remove per-turn memory bootstrap (handled by server)

* feat: replace exec with discover_commands + run_command

- discover_commands: find available commands (prefix/contains filters)
- run_command: execute with workspace restrictions
- cwd limited to ~/.efp/workspace
- Output truncation, timeout handling
- Clean tool schemas with usage examples

* fix: update __init__.py for new discover/run commands

* fix: address Copilot review comments

- #1: Initialize seen set before source blocks
- #2: Use asyncio.to_thread for blocking subprocess
- #3: Set ok=False when exit_code != 0
- #4: Block dangerous commands (rm, mkfs, dd, etc.)
- #5: Block absolute paths and PATH env override
- #6: Make execute_tool async
- #7: Preserve exit_code/stderr in tool result

* docs: update README and config for new discover/run commands

* fix: address additional Copilot review comments

- #9: Check stderr truncation too
- #10: Block more dangerous commands (bash, sudo, shell flags)
- #11/14: Validate cwd exists and is a directory
- #12: Remove unused imports
- #13: Use os.path.isabs() for path check
- #15: Validate args is a list
- #16: Better version error handling
- #17: Fix total_estimate before limit

* fix: address final Copilot review comments

- #20: Fix dangerous_args - only block shell wrapper cmds
- #21: Remove duplicate check
- #22/23: Expand ~ in cwd, update README example

* Update src/bash_tools/api.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add backward compatibility for exec -> run_command mapping

* Update src/bash_tools/api.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update main README with new bash tools (discover/run_command)

refactor(av_quality_monitor): extract helper functions from simulateCall

- Extract simulatePacketTransmission: handles packet transmission with loss patterns (6 lines, 1.3 complexity)
- Extract calculateJitterVariation: adds realistic jitter variation (1 line, 1.3 complexity)
- Extract applyQualityVariation: applies quality degradation for variable profiles (9 lines, 3.1 complexity)
- Extract buildCallMetrics: constructs CallMetrics struct (14 lines, 1.3 complexity)

Metrics:
- simulateCall reduced from 43 lines/11.1 complexity to 17 lines/7.5 complexity (32% complexity improvement)
- All extracted functions meet thresholds (<= 14 lines, <= 3.1 complexity)
- Functions over 30 lines reduced from 114 to 113
- Functions over complexity 10 reduced from 11 to 10
- Code duplication improved: 1.7333% to 1.7325%
- All tests pass with race detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Enhance terminal with full Kali tools simulation

Added simulated commands:
- whois: Domain/IP lookup with realistic output
- dig: DNS queries with MX, NS, A record support

- hydra: Password cracking simulation

- sqlmap: SQL injection testing simulation
- tshark: Packet capture simulation
- msfconsole: Metasploit banner
- ifconfig: Network interface display
- cat: File contents (passwd, etc)

Updated help command with organized categories

commit.message:
chore: bump to v0.8.0

Performance optimization release:
- Parse allocations: pre-sized 7 collections (code blocks, alt text, lists, tables)
- Nav panel: O(n²)→O(n) children computation, O(n)→O(log n) heading search
- Highlight: reduced TextFormat clones in inline code rendering
- Format: single-pass CRLF normalization (was two-pass)
- Render: simplified height estimation, pre-sized image URL allocation
- Dependencies: pulldown-cmark HTML/getopts features removed
- Release profile: opt-level=s→opt-level=2 (10-42% faster across benchmarks)
- Merge: pre-allocated conflict/merge buffers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use PAT_TOKEN in auto-update-prs to trigger unit tests on synchronize

GITHUB_TOKEN pushes don't trigger pull_request workflows (GitHub limitation).
PAT_TOKEN pushes do trigger synchronize event → unit tests run on updated PRs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix schedule mode UI slowdown: eliminate waterfall and heavyweight query (#387)

* Initial plan

* Fix schedule mode UI slowdown by eliminating waterfall and heavyweight query

Replace the heavyweight GET /schedules query (which fetches all schedule
entries just to read the enabled boolean) with the lightweight
GET /schedules/active/page query that already returns both
schedule_enabled and the active page_id.

This eliminates:
1. Unnecessary full schedule list serialization on every page load
2. Request waterfall (two serial API calls reduced to one)

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: switch release profile from opt-level=s to opt-level=2

opt-level=s (size-optimize) traded ~10-42% performance for ~1MB smaller
binary. For a desktop application, prefer speed over binary size:

Benchmark improvements (opt-level=s → opt-level=2):
- height_est_100kb: 105µs → 90µs (15% faster)
- parse_100kb_unicode: 995µs → 805µs (19% faster)
- full_pipeline_100kb: 3.63ms → 3.01ms (17% faster)
- viewport_scan_200kb: 929ns → 537ns (42% faster)
- cache_flip_50kb: 2.11ms → 1.87ms (11% faster)

Binary size: 5.7MB → 6.7MB (still compact with LTO + strip)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Update Supabase client to read from window.ENV runtime config

- Modify supabaseClient.js to prioritize window.ENV over import.meta.env
- This allows Supabase config to work with existing env/env.*.js system
- Add fallback to import.meta.env for development flexibility
- Create .env.local.example for alternative Vite-based config
- Update warning message to mention both config options

Issue: Supabase client was only reading import.meta.env (Vite build-time),
but our project uses env/env.*.js with window.ENV (runtime config)

Solution: Check window.ENV first, then fall back to import.meta.env

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(clippy): suppress aarch64 NEON clippy warnings in weight dequant (#3300)

## What changed
Added file-level clippy allow attributes to neon_weight_dequant.rs and
neon_weight_dequant_v2.rs for manual_is_multiple_of,
needless_range_loop, and related lints.

## Why
macOS ARM64 Clippy job fails with 129 errors on every PR. These files
are aarch64-gated so errors only appear on ARM runners. The pattern is
already established in other NEON kernel files.

## What was tested
- cargo clippy on x86 passes cleanly
- CI will validate on macOS ARM64

## Risks
None - style lints in unsafe NEON intrinsic code.

## Triage: A (MERGE NOW)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add 67 game telemetry integration tests

Add 67 integration tests covering game-specific telemetry parsing,
adapter registration/discovery, packet routing, config generation,
game auto-detection, multi-game concurrency, rate limiting, data
invariants, cross-game normalization consistency, and error handling.

Games tested include Forza Motorsport (UDP binary), Live for Speed
(OutGauge UDP), Rennsport (binary UDP), WRC Generations (Codemasters
Mode 1 UDP), SimHub (JSON UDP), and MudRunner (JSON UDP).

All tests use Result-returning patterns (no unwrap/expect) and
validate adapter behavior with realistic packet formats.

commit.message:
test: add 63 anticheat and audit crypto tests

Add anticheat_hardening_tests.rs with comprehensive test coverage for:

- HMAC-SHA256 correctness against RFC 4231 test vectors (6 cases)
- HMAC-SHA256 edge cases (empty key, block-size key, determinism)
- Audit log entry creation and signing with HMAC-SHA256
- Audit log chain verification (valid chains, wrong key)
- Tamper detection: modified payload, event type, HMAC, sequence,
  timestamp, missing entries, reordered entries, inserted entries
- Audit log rotation with tail hash preservation
- Large chain verification (1000 entries)
- Concurrent audit log access (8 threads × 50 events)
- Anticheat report initialization, serialization roundtrip
- Anticheat report markdown generation and content verification
- Anticheat state machine transitions (valid and invalid)
- Platform-specific anticheat behavior (DLL injection, kernel drivers)
- Game detection integration (telemetry compatibility assertions)
- SHA256 known test vectors
- JSON serialization roundtrip for audit entries
- Unicode and special character payloads

All tests use Result-returning pattern (no unwrap/expect).

commit.message:
Initial release: LabelMe Augmentor v2.0.0

Advanced data augmentation tool for LabelMe annotated datasets with comprehensive
validation and per-class configuration support.

Key Features:
- 40+ Albumentations transforms with mask support
- Per-class augmentation strategies and validation rules
- 9 validation parameters (area, length, shape, border, preservation)
- Angle-independent defect length measurement
- Relative area preservation (works with resize transforms)
- Parallel processing with checkpoint/resume support
- Debug visualizations and comprehensive logging

Validation Parameters:
- Area: min/max_defect_area
- Length: min/max_defect_length (NEW - rotated bounding box)
- Shape: min_contour_points
- Preservation: check_defect_preservation, max_area_change_ratio
- Border: reject_border_defects, border_margin

Technical Highlights:
- Pydantic-based configuration with schema validation
- Type-safe implementation throughout
- 52 unit tests (100% pass rate)
- Compatible with Python 3.8-3.11
- MIT License

Author: Jakhongir Nodirov <jakhon37@gmail.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: scalability improvements for multi-device deployments

- SSE event batching (100ms window) in state.py: prevent notification
  storm when many devices update status simultaneously; reduces SSE
  events by ~10x under mass-reconnect scenarios
- Explicit ThreadPoolExecutor sized to device count (min 64 workers)
  prevents BT reconnect queue starvation at 100+ devices
- D-Bus MessageBus reuse across BT reconnect iterations: no longer
  creates/destroys a new bus connection on every outer loop iteration;
  reconnects only when bus is unresponsive
- Keepalive silence: random startup jitter (0..interval) prevents
  simultaneous paplay bursts across all devices
- _status_monitor_loop sleep increased 2s -> 5s (reduces asyncio
  wakeups from 50/s to 20/s at 100 devices; disconnect still caught
  instantly via D-Bus signals)
- WEB_THREADS env var: configurable Waitress thread count (default 4,
  recommend 16 for deployments with 20+ devices)
- Group-aware pause before BT disconnect stop: solo players receive
  pause signal before sendspin stops; grouped players skip it
- fix: restore missing async def stop_sendspin() declaration
- fix: guard proc.stdin None before write in keepalive burst

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Institutional-grade upgrade: 5-phase signal quality, reliability, and transparency overhaul (#29)

* Initial plan

* feat: comprehensive upgrade — new modules, confluence scoring, session filter, BTC correlation

Part 1 — New bot modules:
- bot/structure_detector.py: fractal swing point detection, dealing range, liquidity levels
- bot/btc_correlation.py: BTC macro bias gate for altcoin signals
- bot/session_filter.py: London/NYC session filter (07-16 and 12-21 UTC)
- bot/vwap.py: VWAP calculator with proximity check
- bot/confluence_score.py: weighted 0-100 confluence scoring across all factors
- bot/chart_generator.py: mplfinance annotated signal chart (graceful fallback)
- bot/dashboard_web.py: Flask performance dashboard with /dashboard, /api/stats, /api/signals
- bot/signal_tracker.py: thread-safe signal lifecycle tracker (TP/SL/BE events)

Part 2 — Existing file modifications:
- bot/logging_config.py: use secrets.token_hex(6) instead of random for signal IDs
- bot/signal_engine.py: add confluence_score field, confidence stars in format_message,
  Bybit copy-trade format, updated confidence logic (FVG+OB+4H direction check)
- bot/ws_manager.py: add 15m candle buffer/stream (backward-compatible has_sufficient_data)
- bot/news_filter.py: add is_stale(), mark_fetch_failed(), fail_safe_window_minutes,
  last_successful_refresh; is_stale() returns False when never loaded (safe default)
- bot/news_fetcher.py: call mark_fetch_failed() when no API key; only load_events() on success
- config.py: add session_filter_enabled, min_confluence_score, news_fail_safe_window_minutes
- requirements.txt: replace sqlalchemy with mplfinance
- .env.example: document new config options

Part 3 — New tests (54 new tests, 271 total):
- tests/test_structure_detector.py
- tests/test_btc_correlation.py
- tests/test_session_filter.py
- tests/test_vwap.py
- tests/test_confluence_score.py
- tests/test_chart_generator.py
- tests/test_integration.py

commit.message:
Switch image gen to fal.ai (FLUX) as primary, Gemini as fallback

- image_gen skill: fal.ai FLUX.1 Schnell (fast) + FLUX Dev (hd quality)
- Falls back to Gemini Nano Banana if fal.ai fails/unavailable
- cli.py: 'ninoclaw imagegen fal <key>' and 'ninoclaw imagegen gemini <key>'
- config.py: FAL_KEY + GEMINI_API_KEY exported
- wizard.py: Step 8 updated with fal.ai as primary option

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: O(log n) active heading search and precomputed children flags

- Replace O(n) linear scan in active_heading_index with binary search
  on sorted byte_offsets (O(log n) for the search + short backward scan)
- Precompute has_visible_children flags during compute_visible_headings
  (single O(n) backward pass instead of O(n²) per render frame)
- Remove standalone has_visible_children function
- Embed has_children bool in cached_visible tuples

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add granular structured logging to service layer functions

Add debug/info/error log statements to functions that previously lacked
logging across five service files:

- attendance_service: get_today_status, get_attendance_history
- financial_service: get_request, get_approved_requests_for_period
- overtime_service: calculate_hourly_rate, get_overtime_for_user,
  get_approved_overtime_for_period (+ error logging for date parse)
- settlement_service: calculate_settlement, get_settlement,
  get_settlements_for_user
- dashboard_service: entry-level debug logs for get_daily_summary,
  get_financial_summary, get_attendance_summary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
